title: Fistula Haemorrhage Control
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- Bleeding from an AV graft or fistula site.

#### Procedure

- Assess for bleeding at the graft or fistula site.

- Assess if patient already has a lid covering the fistula site.

- If not, apply patient issued lid or lid supplied in haemorrhage kit.

- Cover the bleeding site with the hollow part of the lid and press firmly to create a seal.

- Do no remove the lid.

- Once the bleeding is contained in the lid, apply tape over the lid to secure, making sure blood does not spill out of the lid.

- If it begins to bleed outside of the lid, continue with manual pressure.

- If bleeding continues, despite firm pressure, the lid may be too big. Replace with a smaller lid and repeat steps 3 – 6.

- If the bleeding continues despite all above steps and the patient is presenting with signs of life-threatening haemorrhage, apply pressure bandage or tourniquet to control the bleeding as a last resort

- Manage as per CMG 14C Shock and Hypoperfusion as required.

- Transport to TCH with prenotification.

#### Contraindications
- Nil when indicated.

#### Complications
- Nil.

#### Specific Notes
- Fistula site bleeds can cause blood loss of 1-2L per minute.
- The use of pressure bandages and arterial torniquets is **not** recommended in these patients and this can damage the fistula site and limb. This should only be used in cases of severe, life-threatening haemorrhage that can’t be controlled by a lid.
- Even in minor bleeds, these patients must be transported to TCH as they will need vascular intervention. Refer to Patient Destination Guideline.
